title: Document self-hosting workflow for ao-cli
description: Define how this repository uses ao to plan, track, and execute its own roadmap, including required commands and state files.
acceptance_criteria: README contains an end-to-end self-hosting section with concrete ao commands.
AGENTS.md defines operating rules for agents contributing to this repo.

title: Stabilize core CLI package ergonomics
description: Improve command discoverability, help text quality, and error messaging for the orchestrator-cli command groups used daily.
acceptance_criteria: Core command groups (project, requirements, task, workflow, execute) have concise and consistent help output.
Common user input errors return actionable messages with suggested fixes.

title: Harden agent-runner lifecycle and observability
description: Ensure runner startup, health checks, and shutdown paths are robust across local development workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Runner startup and shutdown behavior is covered by repeatable tests.
Runner health failure paths emit structured diagnostics.

title: Mature llm-cli-wrapper and llm-mcp-server integration
description: Standardize wrapper <-> MCP server behavior for build, startup, endpoint resolution, and smoke validation.
acceptance_criteria: Wrapper can build and start llm-mcp-server using documented commands.
Integration example validates health and agent endpoint responses.

title: Establish CI checks for non-Tauri workspace
description: Create CI coverage focused on the Rust-only workspace packages and smoke command execution.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs cargo check for orchestrator-cli, orchestrator-core, agent-runner, llm-cli-wrapper, llm-mcp-server, and web server crates.
CI validates help/smoke commands for ao and llm-cli-wrapper.

title: Add release packaging strategy for multi-binary workspace
description: Define and implement reproducible build artifacts for ao, agent-runner, llm-cli-wrapper, and llm-mcp-server.
acceptance_criteria: Release process documents artifact naming, target platforms, and verification steps.
At least one automated build path produces all required binaries.
